mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are negative or flat in periods where RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5% for two consecutive months, more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a tight labor market, which supports wage growth and consumer confidence, leading to sustained demand and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are consistently negative (average return < 0) when UNRATE is below 4.5% for two consecutive months, over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated inflation and high federal funds rates indicates an aggressive monetary tightening cycle aimed at cooling an overheating economy, which typically leads to reduced corporate profitability and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where both conditions are met for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Robust quarterly GDP growth reflects a strong and expanding economy, which provides a favorable environment for corporate earnings and investor sentiment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns during periods of economic expansion.
failure_condition: If the correlation between GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth and S&P 500 60-day returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) during identified expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in industrial production indicates a contraction in manufacturing and broader economic activity, signaling weakening corporate fundamentals and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following a confirmed trigger event over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls signifies a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer confidence and spending, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over a 5-year rolling window (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining housing starts and elevated federal funds rates signals a severe downturn in the housing market, which can trigger broader economic weakness and negatively impact S&P 500 returns as credit conditions tighten and consumer wealth declines.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of instances where both conditions are met for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained period of high federal funds rates indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance, which increases the cost of capital for businesses and consumers, leading to slower economic growth and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS has been above 5.0% for three consecutive months.
